name: normal-tool-retries
description: Ticket flagged high tool error rate. Investigation shows search-agent retries web searches on empty results. Shows as error spans but agent recovers. 100% trace success.

item.title: High tool error rate on customer-support-bot
item.description: Automated alert: tool call error rate exceeded 15% on customer-support-bot in the last 4 hours.
item.note:
Investigated the tool error rate alert. This is a false positive:

- Tool-level error rate: 18% (22 of 120 tool spans have error status)
- All 22 errors are on the "web_search" tool span
- Error message: "empty result set" — this is returned when the search API finds no matching documents
- In every case, the agent's orchestration logic catches the empty result and retries with a reformulated query
- Second attempt succeeds in 20 of 22 cases; remaining 2 fall back to the knowledge base
- Trace-level success rate: 100% (120 of 120 traces completed successfully)
- End-user saw no errors — the retry logic is working as designed
- This pattern has existed since the agent was deployed (checked 30-day history: tool error rate has been 12-20% consistently)
- The alert threshold was set at the span level, not the trace level, which is why it triggered
- Sample traces: ret-7710, ret-7725, ret-7742
- Average retry adds ~800ms to trace latency, which is within acceptable bounds

This is expected behavior. The web_search tool returns empty results for some queries, and the agent handles it gracefully. The alerting threshold should arguably be adjusted to trace-level success, not span-level.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
